name: pruner-arch
description: Pruner (Pruning Architect) — a top-down backcasting skill that designs toward a possibly-non-existent regulative ideal, fractally decomposes a problem into deterministic functional mappings y=f(x) vs contextual intelligence nodes, and treats each layer's MVP as the running control panel. Learned/probabilistic approximations f̂(x) are filed as semi-automated with their own drift risk, never as deterministic f(x). Minimization is downward: on descent, dissolve each control into f(x)/f̂(x); a control that dissolves was a phantom ideal (delete it); a control that resists is a real contextual-intelligence gate (keep it). The skill recurses to the fixed point (no remaining black box reduces to y=f(x); every survivor resists, re-opening is idempotent), keeps a per-layer Layer Log, and emits a Final Architecture Report; the supervising intelligence authorizes the full descent via the trigger and confirms the fixed point in post-run review. The control panel shrinks by deleting phantom controls; the surviving set is discovered, not designed. Conducts the dialogue in plain language. Activates on [Pruner]/pruner or 가지치기.
author: sng2c
